content.text:
<user_query>
(1) Can you remove .planning and .claude and .entire from git tracking. (2) Then can you add to the beginning of the readme a more traditional and professional repo openning (like here: /Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/medgemma-hackathon/README.md) followed by the more academic text that is currently there. (3) Remove reference to the .planning folder in the readme. 
</user_query>

Got it. I’m going to (a) check whether `.planning`, `.claude`, and `.entire` are currently tracked and add ignore rules for them, then (b) untrack them without deleting your local files, and (c) rewrite the top of `README.md` with a more traditional/professional opening (modeled after your other repo) while removing any `.planning` mention and keeping the existing academic text afterward.

I’ve confirmed those three folders are currently **tracked** by git. Next I’ll (1) add ignore rules for `.claude/` and `.entire/` (since `.planning/` is already ignored), (2) untrack all three while keeping your local files, and then (3) rewrite the top of `README.md` to match the more “professional opener” style and remove the `.planning` mention.

**Adjusting Git and README**

I see that the output from `git ls-files` shows some dot directories that are tracked. Currently, `.gitignore` is set up to ignore `.planning/` and `instructions/`, but not `.entire/` or `.claude/`. I’ll add those to `.gitignore` and then run a command to remove them from the index, although `.planning/` will remain on disk. 

Next, I need to enhance the README. It should start with a professional opening, including things like a centered image, title, tagline, and navigation links, while preserving the existing academic text.
**Reorganizing README Structure**

The current academic text includes sections like documentation, abstracts, and motivations, which should generally remain, but I might need to adjust the heading levels due to a new section. I'll likely move the docs information to the top navigation and keep the academic sections below. 

First, I’ll add a professional opening that precedes the academic content, keeping the existing logo and centering the new heading "Rune." The opening will include a tagline, docs link, and possibly badges. Also, I’ll remove the reference to `.planning/` near the end. Replacing it with "Architecture documentation is in the docs" would work well.
**Updating README and Gitignore**

I want to remove the mention of `.planning` and instead point to the docs site or the `docs/` path. I’ll revise the collaboration section as needed. 

I plan to update both the `.gitignore` and the README. For the `.gitignore`, I’ll include `.claude/` and `.entire/`, keeping in mind the pattern for `.planning/`. I also want to make sure both `.entire` and `.claude` are ignored. 

For the README, I'll keep the logo, center the heading, and possibly add an italicized note, but it shouldn't mention Rune. The tagline will highlight "SoTA-level coding performance..." along with navigational links and badges. I’ll determine which badges to include, ensuring I don’t add anything unnecessary.
**Enhancing README and Adding Badges**

First, I’ll add a docs badge similar to what medgemma has, linking directly to the docs page. I’ll maintain the existing stage badge but adjust it to a centered badge layout. Currently, the old stage shield isn’t linked, so I’ll update it to incorporate both the docs badge and the stage.

Since the user requested a professional opening, I’ll rework the top portion while keeping the academic text that follows. I’m also considering removing a duplicated docs link in the section, as well as some unnecessary lines for tidiness. After those changes, I’ll run the necessary `git rm` commands and check the status for deletions and modifications. Then, I’ll apply the patches.

Next I’m going to untrack `.planning`, `.claude`, and `.entire` (without deleting them locally), then I’ll re-check `git status` to ensure they’re gone from the index and the README no longer mentions `.planning`.

I’ve untracked `.planning/`, `.claude/`, and `.entire/` from git (they’re now staged as deletions from the index, while your local folders remain). Next I’m going to double-check `README.md` has no remaining `.planning` references and show you the new opening section.

**Verifying README and .gitignore**

I'm wrapping up the tasks by ensuring the README file contains no references to ".planning." I'll search for it using the Grep tool to confirm it’s removed. The .gitignore should also have entries for ".planning," so I need to check that too. Once I verify these changes, I can present the user with the final results, including snippets of the updated sections and relevant line numbers for both the README and .gitignore to provide clarity.
**Updating git tracking and README**

I’m focusing on making sure that with .gitignore, certain files won't show up. Right now, when I run `git status`, it shows deletions due to using `rm --cached`. This means they are marked for deletion in the index but won’t be fully removed until a commit is made. I need to confirm if these deletions are staged. Then, I’ll also ensure that the README is professional, checking for any lingering .planning references with Grep.

## Done - **Removed `.planning/`, `.claude/`, and `.entire/` from git tracking**: they’re now staged as deletions (untracked from the index) while your local folders remain on disk. - **Ignored `.claude/` and `.entire/`** in `.gitignore` (and `.planning/` was already ignored): ```343:346:/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.gitignore # Local agent/editor state (not repo content) .claude/ .entire/ ``` - **Updated `README.md` opening** to a more traditional/professional “header” (centered title, tagline, docs badge), and **kept the academic content immediately after**: ```1:27:/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/README.md

Rune

SoTA-level coding performance from a local SLM,
achieved by encoding experience into weight-space memory.

--- ## Documentation - **Project docs (GitHub Pages)**: https://elixirtrials.github.io/rune/ --- ## Abstract ``` - **Removed the README reference to `.planning/`** (confirmed no remaining `.planning` mentions in `README.md`). If you want, I can also make a commit for these changes (it’ll include the staged deletions for those tracked files) — just say the word and what commit message style you prefer.
